name: detect-picker-owns-input-arabic-indic-digit
comment: Same historical parity break as the readyForInput digit-family cases: an over-eager digit check (e.g. Python str.isdigit()) treats an Arabic-Indic digit as numeric and would wrongly count this row as a numbered menu option. The correct (ASCII-only) check in both languages does not, so no picker is reported open - cursor is absent, so if the digit check were wrong this would flip to true.
